provenance.note: Values are normalized from the listed Palworld 1.0 DataTables. Site recommendations are derived separately.

Lamball.description: A walk up a hill tends to end with this Pal tumbling back down. This causes it to become dizzy and unable to move, making it easy to capture and kill. As a result, this Pal has tumbled down to the very bottom of the food chain itself.

Cattiva.description: At a glance it appears full of confidence, but it is in fact weak and cowardly. Being toyed with by a is in many ways the greatest of disgraces.

Chikipi.description: Extremely weak and far too delicious. It is one of the weakest Pals alongside . No matter how many are hunted, they just keep appearing.

Lifmunk.description: Intelligent as a five to seven-year-old human child. It makes a wonderful partner, but there have been more than a few cases where they've killed their master after learning to use weapons.

Fuack.description: Using its own body water, this Pal can create waves anywhere. It body surfs when in a hurry, but the resulting speed often ends in a fatal collision.

Vixy.description: The idol of Palpagos Island. If you bully a , you best be prepared to become enemies with the whole world.

Celaray.description: Riding the wind, this Pal travels where it pleases. Should it find a partner along the way, the encounter will mark its journey's end.

Cremis.description: Compared to , it has finer wool and a temperament more suited for domestication. However, it has historically always been kept as a pet. Cuteness is considered a virtue.

Croajiro.description: Those who disgrace the honor of the herd are forced to take their own life. By piercing their inflated vocal sac with a tree branch, they are blown away into the atmosphere and return to the afterlife.

Herbil.description: When in doubt, punch it till it works—that’s 's approach to fixing things. With strength perfectly tuned for humans, sometimes just one punch is enough to restart a stopped heart.

Teafant.description: Large amounts of water pour from what is thought to be its nose, though some say that it is, in fact, just snot. This has sparked a fierce debate among Pal scholars.

Gumoss.description: A strange Pal with a body resembling tree sap. It gradually loses strength if it has nothing to cover its body with, eventually rotting away.

Pupperai.description: territorial disputes begin with a staredown and end in a single, quick strike. Training them to |stay| is risky business, as the |go| command triggers that same attack instinct.

Clovee.description: Some individuals once had three or more leaves on their foreheads. Regarded as symbols of good fortune, those with more leaves were heavily hunted, leading the species to evolve fewer leaves over time.

Jolthog.description: It releases the electricity stored in its body when under attack. The voltage it generates exceeds 10 million volts, making it more lethal than any conventional heavy firearms when thrown.

Depresso.description: It has few friends because of the perpetually grouchy look on its face, but it is in fact kindhearted. Some have seen it feeding who have strayed from their pack.

Pengullet.description: The feathers of this Pal have all but disappeared, but sadly, its desire to fly has remained as strong as ever. Even now, it tries to fly again in any way it can.

Penking.description: Surprisingly, it is unrelated to . However, since this Pal gets pampered as it pleases, for now, it's doing its best to strut arrogantly.

Hoocrates.description: Often lost in thought, it sometimes finds it difficult to sleep. |I think, therefore I am.|

Melpaca.description: Don't be fooled by this Pal's fluffy appearance. A hypersonic kick from one of its long legs may send you flying to the other side of the world.

Kingpaca.description: serve this Pal. Contests between offer up their vassals as a wager. Those seen alone are losers of such contests.

Daedream.description: It puts those it is interested in to sleep and shows them an endless stream of happy dreams. Those who fall under its spell are never to wake until death takes them.

Tanzee.description: Long ago, this Pal used long objects like tree branches as weapons. After coming into contact with humans, however, it found something slightly more effective: guns.

Nox.description: If you find hair in your bedding, you should leave it where it lays and leave immediately. Picking it up is a one-way-ticket to a never ending night.

Flambelle.description: When it starts crying, this Pal produces magma in place of tears. The magma that pours out is absorbed back into its body, causing it to get hotter and hotter. The more it cries, the stronger it becomes.

Rooby.description: Wild surprisingly never get sick. Eating one piece of charcoal a day, made by burning a branch, is the secret to its eternal health.

Mau.description: Its hard tail does not deteriorate even when cut off. Some believed these severed tails bring good luck, but for the innumerable who were poached as a result, they were anything but.

Mau Cryst.description: Its crystalline tail is beautiful, but shatters when this Pal dies. Some believe it is good luck to raise them, so in captivity are treated with great care.

Rushoar.description: Being an extremely aggressive Pal, it often picks fights before gauging its opponents' strength. Though small, its powerful charge can even send boulders flying.

Foxparks.description: It is unskilled at controlling fire from the moment it is born and tends to choke on the flames it breathes unintentionally. sneezes are one of the leading causes of forest fires.

Killamari.description: It wraps itself around an enemy's head, sucking out their insides. Pal mummies are occasionally found, but these are in fact victims.

Fuddler.description: Its large claws boast of diamond-like hardness. However, sharpening these claws consumes most of its energy, leading it to sometimes spending entire days doing nothing else.

Eikthyrdeer.description: The one who possesses the most impressive antlers becomes the leader of the herd. If their antlers are broken, they become depressed and leave the herd never to be seen again.

Direhowl.description: Long ago would hunt alongside humans but over the years this bond faded.

Caprity.description: The shrub on this Pal's back produces berries as long as it is properly fed. It offers these berries to potential mates, and if the flavor is to their liking, romance blossoms.

Swee.description: Crawling along the ground, it eats microscopic organic matter. After a while, it discharges any substances that provide no nutrition. By using it as a mop for cleaning, everyone wins!

Sweepa.description: While hibernating, a large number of hide within its voluminous body hair. The most ever recorded is 101.

Turtacle.description: To protect its fragile body, it developed an incredibly tough shell. Unfortunately, it's utterly defenseless underneath. Even a little tickle could knock it out cold.

Hangyu.description: Its gigantic arms can rip apart even iron. As a particularly cruel form of execution, serious criminals would be strung up in a public square, and a would tear the skin right from their bones.

Woolipop.description: Its entire body is 18,000 times sweeter than sugar. Carnivorous Pals lured by its scent will find themselves overwhelmed by sweetness and even pass out should they take a bite of this Pal.

Mozzarina.description: Milk pours from this Pal like water from an open faucet, regardless of its gender. It's truly a mystery of life, although this mystery may be better left unsolved.

Azurobe.description: This Pal's white ribbon turns black if doused with impure water. Given its usefulness in detecting poison, this Pal was once overhunted. This past has left them bitter towards humanity.

Sparkit.description: During the dry season, this Pal is always on the verge of blowing a fuse. Sparks can fly with even the slightest provocation, even amongst allies.

Kelpsea.description: Its personality changes depending on the quality of the water it was born into. born into polluted waters are generally ill-tempered and quickly become delinquents.

Ribbuny.description: A Pal that's never without a bright smile. Occasionally, its tentacles get tied up in knots by 's pranks. During those moments, its expression turns demonic.

Jelliette.description: A Pal that closely resembles . It’s very social, so when plans overlap, it asks its good friend to go in its place. As thanks, one date.

Jellroy.description: A Pal that closely resembles . It sometimes appears as a substitute when is unavailable. Its acting is so flawless that no one ever notices the switch.

Amione.description: If one disappears, another simply shows up to take its place, and life goes on like nothing happened. For , companionship is everything.

Gloopie.description: Be sure to check your plans for the next day before falling asleep with this Pal. Its suction cups leave marks that look suspiciously like hickeys. One sleepy cuddle session could lead to some truly awkward misunderstandings.

Galeclaw.description: A Pal that can easily take flight even while grasping a human. It is, however, prone to letting go when tired, which has led to the sudden demise of more than a few souls.

Wispaw.description: The ghostly hands behind it embody the souls of all humans who have raised its kind. Lately, they seem to have reached a unanimous consensus to dote on .

Nitewing.description: It carries newborn Pals to its nest and raises them as a surrogate parent. Once the baby Pal has fattened up, it hunts them.

Tombat.description: Often appears out of the blue to flaunt its prized wings in front of other Pals. Although this appears to be an intimidation tactic, the Pal seems to derive some kind of pleasure from the display.

Tocotoco.description: A frightening Pal that produces exploding eggs. It often fires these eggs from its rear end as a form of defense, but when spent, the Pal itself explodes.

Univolt.description: It used to be considered an emissary of the Thunder God, and thus was not hunted by people. However, after witnesses observed one die from a lightning strike, its reverence faded, and it quite literally fell into the role of a workhorse.

Gobfin.description: Long ago, it was a giant and powerful aquatic Pal. However, as food became scarce, it evolved to live on land. Since walking requires much energy, it gradually became smaller and is now a small and puny Pal.

Loupmoon.description: The horns on its head grow under the moonlight. It doesn't hate the sun, but its horns itch when sunlight hits them.

Cawgnito.description: Long ago it freely soared the skies. After losing a contest with , it abandoned the skies, and now lives a secretive life in the dark of night.

Arsox.description: In ancient times, carnivorous Pals pursued them relentlessly. The absurd fury in the cries of transformed into a raging inferno, which has been passed on to this day.

Muffly.description: Its soft facial fluff softens any hit, but it cannot groom itself well nor scratch its itches, leaving it in a constant state of mild frustration.

Bristla.description: This prickly Pal's thorns are highly poisonous. It is friendly with and only smiles while a is drinking its nectar.

Cinnamoth.description: Sniffing its scales produces a feeling of unparalleled euphoria. There was some effort to further regulate this byproduct, but the Free Pal Alliance have vehemently opposed these measures, putting a stop to them.

Puffolt.description: Its fluffy fur absorbs tremendous amounts of water when soaked. Shaking it dry releases a powerful electrical discharge, making bath time together a serious gamble.

Elphidran.description: It possesses a demeanor as pure as its appearance suggests. Perhaps because of this, it is sometimes unable to discern good from evil, often allowing wrongdoers to take advantage of it.

Vanwyrm.description: The melodies of a flute made from the exoskeleton of a are said to cross whole mountain ranges. In ages past, such flutes were used to signal an attack.

Felbat.description: Attacking from the shadows, this Pal traps its prey within its cloak-like wings. It's probably best not to know what happens within them, or why the inside of its wings are stained red.

Vaelet.description: The castle was filled with the king's favorite flowers. A great battle ensued, and flames approached the castle. Amidst the chaos, the spirit of a flower appeared. - From the fairy tale, |The King's Flower.|

Beegarde.description: A servant that pledges loyalty to . Any that cause harm to the queen are immediately expelled from the hive. It will gladly give its life to protect its queen.

Elizabee.description: A chosen queen to rule over s. There is a never ending stream of servants willing to work themselves to death for the pleasure of serving their queen.

Lovander.description: Seeking a night of love, it is always chasing someone around. At first it only showed interest in other Pals, but in recent years even humans have become the target of its debauchery.

Grintale.description: 's eyes light up the moment anyone enters its territory. This is no figure of speech—its eyes literally light up.

Tarantriss.description: Extremely sticky and clingy. The more it's resisted, the more tenacious it becomes. Such is its |personality.|

Polapup.description: Among s, the fastest slider on the snow earns the highest respect. Sometimes, a too-forceful leap sends one tumbling from the sky— a common sight during territorial season. Forecast: clear skies with a chance of flying s.

Leezpunk.description: A Pal that always takes great care to maintain a stylish stance. Always on the hunt for the coolest poses, if given a mirror it will spend all day posing in front of it.

Gorirat.description: It beats the ground rhythmically to communicate with its comrades. The meaning of each rhythm differs by troop, but the distinction between them is still largely unknown.

Surfent.description: Its hydrodynamic form is well suited for activities on water. Poachers often catch them and use them in place of surfboards.

Robinquill.description: A Pal that is very similar to humans who hunt and live in the forests. It may prove to be a key for understanding what Pals are and how they diverged from humans in the distant past.

Flopie.description: It prefers places that have an abundance of vegetation, but hay fever has apparently become a problem for as of late.

Wixen.description: With the power of light, it produces arcane phenomena. It prefers to eat food well-done and is particularly hostile towards .

Wixen Noct.description: Due to its upbringing, it no longer cares about whether its food is cooked enough. This Pal stopped worrying about those details, and just prefers its food to be as sweet as possible. Not on good terms with .

Katress.description: With the power of shadows, it produces arcane phenomena. It prefers to eat food raw and is particularly hostile towards .

Helzephyr.description: It calls forth lightning from the depths of hell. Those who dies from 's lightning are sure to have their soul sent to the underworld.

Elgrove.description: The leaf on its horn cannot be removed, no matter how hard it is pulled. When the leaf falls on its own, accepts its time has come.

Lunaris.description: It can control those who carelessly stare into its eyes. Those seen with a are in its mind, simply under its control.

Fenglope.description: In ages past, its beautiful visage was a common sight in paintings. As time passed, its beautiful pelt and antlers were often seen in works of art.

Dinossom.description: A Pal who once angered cannot be pacified. It rages on and on like an inferno. The phrase |step on a 's tail| has come to mean enraging someone.

Bushi.description: Its body becomes a blade upon death, to be taken up by the next generation. If someone other than a wields this blade, the soul within torments them until they are driven mad.

Munchill.description: The inside of its mouth stays pleasantly cold, making it a natural fridge when closed. Its saliva contains enzymes that prevent food from rotting. Thus, it can serve as a food locker for humans— whether it is sanitary or not is another matter entirely.

Mammorest.description: The vegetation on its back varies between individuals. There is a long history of appreciating this veritable garden of a Pal, and there are even pruning specialists.

Finsider.description: It looks like something is peeking out from inside a costume, but even if you reach inside, there’s nothing there. In fact, you can keep reaching deeper and deeper. Perhaps the universe itself lies within.

Petallia.description: A Pal that transforms into a massive plant when at the end of its life. Once every ten years, a beautiful flower blooms and a new is born.

Leafan.description: To hone the martial art passed down through a single bloodline: Daily training is the only way! Being distracted by romance? Unthinkable! Daily discipline is the only way! The time has come! Shoot my shot! Instant rejection, that’s all there is! The techniques of love were never passed down... Lingering regret, that’s all there is!

Incineram.description: In the dark of night, this Pal snatches prey to bring back to its territory. What happens to those poor souls afterwards isn't too hard to imagine.

Dazzi.description: Often kind to lonely Pals. However, the moment a Pal mistakes this for actual friendship, it seizes the opportunity to blast them with a thunderbolt.

Pyrin.description: Its entire body has evolved into a highly efficient radiator, gifting it with astounding stamina. If someone rides it, this Pal takes caution not to burn them.

Relaxaurus.description: Contrary to its blasé appearance, it's quite ferocious. It perceives everything in its sight as prey and will stop at nothing to devour it.

Foxcicle.description: On nights when the aurora is visible, it looks up towards the sky and begins to howl a beautiful song. This does, however, leave it vulnerable to attacks from enemies.

Beakon.description: Some think it is a related species to , but there is in fact no connection. Using its sharp beak, it descends on its prey in a quick motion that resembles a bolt of lightning.

Ghangler.description: With its shining lure, it illuminates the darkness. The wandering Pals drawn in by the light stand no chance of escape once spotted by . No matter how far, it will keep guiding them until they get home safe and sound.

Rayhound.description: At full speed, it could be mistaken for a bolt of lightning. When two s collide, a thunderous roar echoes through the air.

Menasting.description: Being made of pure energy, its insides are completely hollow. This Pal crams still-living prey into its hollow body, where it absorbs them. Hellish screams of pain can often be heard coming from inside this Pal.

Needoll.description: Its spikes react to its emotions. A dearly cherished is something one may instinctively want to embrace. But more often than not, it ends in a tragic accident.

Reindrix.description: Its transparent cerulean antlers glow with the cold of absolute zero. Any who touch them with their bare hands are instantly frozen and smashed to pieces.

Mossanda.description: A Pal so powerful it's hard to believe. In one experiment, this Pal tore through 3,000 sheets of paper at once! It's only by some miracle that this Pal isn't a meat-eater.

Chillet.description: It can curl up its body and roll around at extremely high speeds. Long ago, people would tie bags of milk to domesticated as they grazed to produce butter using this spinning force.

Ragnahawk.description: Some think it is a related species to , but there is in fact no connection. It mainly eats rocks, and after many long years its beak and head have hardened to accommodate this diet.

Moldron.description: In a healthy individual, the lava on its back flows smoothly. However, a poor lifestyle will gradually worsen its circulation, eventually causing a lava clog that results in a sudden, massive explosion.

Palumba.description: Disrupts enemies’ rhythm with a signature dance. In turf battles, the first to fall in sync with the other’s rhythm loses. Courtship, boredom, battle—it all comes down to dance! Now then, what could a dance aimed at a human possibly mean?

Digtoise.description: A possessing the strongest shell and the only drill capable of piercing it, ponders the contradictions of the world in the popular children's tale, |'s Fable|.

Broncherry.description: Its scent drastically changes before and after pairing. It exudes a pleasing aroma after finding a partner, which is called the |Fragrance of First Love.|

Dumud.description: When too relaxed, its reaction time drastically declines. Even if it were sliced from head to tail, it probably wouldn't even realize that it should be dead until the next morning.

Braloha.description: Its breath carries a miraculous, pollen-like substance that pollinates any plant it touches. For plant-based Pals, being near it can be a bit… complicated.

Kitsun.description: Despite its appearance, is extremely sensitive and will flee into a cave when spooked. Long ago, it was considered an ill omen if one ran away from you.

Blazehowl.description: While it prefers raw meat, it always ends up eating well-done meat. This is due to its blistering claws, which it uses as its weapon—it simply doesn't realize its prey gets burned to a crisp.

Warsect.description: The ultra-hard armor surrounding its body is extremely strong and heat resistant. Even a napalm blast would hardly leave a scratch.

Frostplume.description: So precise is its sense of time that it awakens at the exact same hour every morning. According to Palpagos lore, a day was divided into 24 parts because when spreads its tail feathers wide, it reveals a pattern with 24 distinct spaces.

Majex.description: It appears to be striking a heroic pose, but the reality is that it's simply bracing itself against the weight of its tail and hair to avoid toppling over.

Sibelyx.description: A Pal that likes the rain, and will often bask in rain showers until the weather clears up. On rainy days, can often be found taking shelter beneath it.

Maraith.description: It relishes the peculiar scent living things give off when they are near death. If a has taken a liking to you, it is safe to assume that is why.

Shroomer.description: Inhaling large amounts of its spores will put you into a trance. Sometimes, if appears motionless, it's not because it's mimicking a mushroom, but rather because it's high off its own spores.

Icelyn.description: Though its body melts in the heat, it just can’t resist sunbathing. Though its body melts in the warmth, it likes hot springs too much. If it's going to melt anyway, it hopes it’ll be in a steaming outdoor bath under a clear blue sky.

Gildra.description: The golden, bandage-like organs covering its body are highly elastic. However, they are prone to tearing under extremely dry conditions, so daily moisture checks are absolutely essential.

Jormuntide.description: Legend says the was once a wise man who, after being wrongly convicted and cast into a whirlpool, returned as this Pal to annihilate the kingdom.

Suzaku.description: It was once believed to usher in the dry season. Whenever there was a drought the previous year, people would relentlessly seek to cull its numbers, hoping to bring about a plentiful harvest in the next year.

Dazemu.description: Take one step forward and forget your doubts. Take two steps forward and forget your worries. Take three steps forward and forget your fears. Face any enemy.

Quivern.description: Sleeping while cuddling a is said to be a heavenly experience, but there are some who have been crushed and sent to heaven by ones that toss and turn in their sleep.

Lullu.description: In early spring, pollen floats on the wind throughout the island. 's hay fever is 's fault.

Kikit.description: A decade ago, soccer was popular. However, it quickly fell out of favor due to fierce opposition from the Free Pal Alliance. Nevertheless, the 's themselves didn't mind because it helped shed their old carapace.

Yakumo.description: It has a strong sense of justice and despises evil. In the past they were faithful and loving companions to the islanders. Nowadays, s are hostile to humans wherever they spot them.

Skutlass.description: A bizarre species which temporarily separates its body and uses its bones to slice through enemies, at the risk of perishing itself upon taking too long. While unconfirmed, older individuals are said to deliberately stay separated in order to trigger their survival instincts and prolong their lifespans.

Reptyro.description: Magma-like blood runs throughout its body. If a large amount of water is thrown on it, the water rapidly heats, causing an immense vapor explosion.

Starryon.description: It takes great pride in its flowing, gorgeous mane. If it finds a split end, it becomes so mortified it can't focus on running properly. Because static electricity makes its hair frizzy, it avoids at all costs.

Pierdon.description: It smashes its body against harder rocks to maintain its preferred shape. Those with noticeable dents are best left alone, much like people with a botched haircut.

Cryolinx.description: It can easily climb steep mountains with its hard claws. However, its short legs make it difficult to descend, often leaving it stranded in high places.

Snugloo.description: “To look at a 's eyes” is an expression which means to seek the unseen essence of things. Ironically, no researcher has ever confirmed whether s have eyes in the first place.

Wumpo.description: Researchers once tried to shave off its hair to reveal its true form. In the end, only hair was left, as if that was all there was to begin with.

Sootseer.description: It was once believed that as the world approached its end, the flame would weaken. Doomsayers would repeatedly rejoice when the flame began to weaken, but in the end, it turned out to be a prank by . At least, for now.

Carnibora.description: Prone to eating indiscriminately, the mouth atop its head frequently breaks out in agonizing ulcers, causing it to use the mouth on its face instead. However, its behavior doesn't change, which leads to both mouths suffering the same ulcers.

Blazamut.description: Legends say it was born during a volcanic eruption. A strange group even claims that this continent is laid upon the back of a giant .

Dualith.description: One entity, two minds. When their wills clash, it's impossible to tell where the armor will wander until they reconcile.

Anubis.description: Once seen as a symbol of nobility, and an idol for those who shunned wealth and power. Yet over time, this Pal became a token of death.

Sekhmet.description: Once a fierce creature feared as a symbol of death. When claimed that mantle, wealthy elites looking for a replacement beguiled the species with wine. Before long, became a symbol of high nobility.

Prixter.description: It has about 5,000 types of venom. Some of them are deadly enough to cause instant death, but none are present in a high enough concentration to have any effect.

Tetroise.description: Those stones on its back are not just cargo. Set them down somewhere distant and they will slowly make their way back home.

Nyafia.description: Strongly dislikes anyone catching a glimpse of its concealed eye. If you insist on trying, it'll burst into tears and ignore you for two weeks.

Mimog.description: There was once a tradition of handing out an award to the person who met their end in the most foolish way. The first award was given for |being unaware of , opening the chest, and being bitten to death.|

Xenovader.description: Can send information and convert it into energy by forgetting it. Regularly seems to be sending information to someone. - Respond. Target located. Proceed to immediate destruction.

Xenogard.description: Consumes information and turns it into energy. Regularly seems to be sending information to someone. - Hello? What was the message you just sent? I devoured it before checking.

Prunelia.description: Intelligent and graceful in its demeanor, it was frequently employed as an attendant by the island's nobles. However, when encountered at night, its face looked so eerie that it quickly fell out of favor.

Nitemary.description: What it carries is its own soul. When it falls for someone, it forcibly extracts their soul and merges it with its own, yearning to become one.

Smokie.description: The black flames are merely warm to the touch, but anything left in contact with them slowly melts from the inside. Hold some cheese over them, and it becomes a true delicacy.

Omascul.description: The mask it wears is not its face, they say. It seems in front, but it's not there, no way. From shadows deep, it watches night and day. Glance away, and closer it will stray.

Whalaska.description: When it sleeps, Pals often mistake it for a drifting piece of ice and rest on it. Although gentle and unwilling to push them away when awake, it feels quietly stressed by the intrusion on its personal space.

Verdash.description: Land that has run across becomes extremely fertile, with thick vegetation growing soon after. It will not run anywhere that herbicide has been used.

Splatterina.description: It has an uncontrollable urge to tear its prey apart. Each individual has their own meticulous order for dismemberment. If caught by , pray that your head is taken first.

Gildane.description: During the golden age of alchemy on the island, its ever-glowing golden fur was highly prized as a catalyst for transmuting gold. In time, a remarkable revelation was made: the same golden hue could be achieved simply by processing its mane.

Dogen.description: Striving for a state of selflessness, it meditates endlessly. After confronting its worldly desires, it eats a meal and reflects. It ate too much today.

Bulldosu.description: In the old islands, sumo was widely enjoyed. At the highest levels, even a charging would be sent flying instead.

Celesdir.description: Ancient texts refer to it as the |Beast of Salvation.| The light emitted by purifies everything, literally causing things to vanish from existence without leaving a trace.

Astegon.description: A savage beast born of the abyss. Thou shall not stand before the beast. Thou shall not heed the beast.

Knocklem.description: Can be disassembled into 215 parts. It is capable of being reassembled and functioning again even if the parts are scattered. Mixing parts from different individuals will trigger a malfunction due to mistaken identity.

Silvegis.description: The metallic components around its neck possess magnetic properties. If the leg pieces get too close, they cling to it. When metal debris builds up on its neck, it grudgingly begins cleaning, and grows frustrated when they won't come off easily.

Azurmane.description: Like , it was once believed to be connected to the Thunder God. As faith in waned, was deemed the God's true envoy. Even so, time reduced both to the same lowly status of ordinary workhorses.

Valentail.description: In older paintings, it is depicted as much smaller than it is today. For reasons unknown, various parts of its body have begun to elongate rapidly in recent years, and it is predicted to reach nearly ten times its current size within the next century.

Snock.description: Tossing an into the bath for a shock spa is considered a traditional health practice. That is to say, the results may be rather shocking.

Souffline.description: Its fluff can be used to hide away unpleasant memories. Once carried off by the wind, you can bid farewell to the darkness they carried.

Lapiron.description: Listens to the earth as it runs, avoiding unstable footing with ease. However, uncooperative terrain will trick it into falling over constantly.

Hoodle.description: When it sleeps, it deflates. When it sleeps, it's no longer there. A sleeping cannot exist; Therefore, never sleeps.

Slowatt.description: Doing as they please, sleeping when they wish, and eating whatever they like. Surely, must be toiling in secret to maintain such a luxury. ...Or so the old islanders told themselves, just to get by.

Bakemi.description: It lives entirely for the thrill of the scare, but is utterly un-frightening. As most cannot help but indulge it with feigned surprise for its cuteness, now leaves a trail of smiles in its wake.

Solmora.description: Its corkscrew tail acts as a propeller. Research claims that if its body could withstand the strain, it could reach speeds beyond 88 mph.

Lapure.description: It uses the long, flowing fur on its hands to attract attention, always taking the utmost care to keep them maintained. Highly sensitive to dirt and meticulously clean, kicking up dust nearby will invite a very disgusted glare.

Eidrolon.description: A new Pal that was only recently discovered. Calm and entirely docile, it spends its time as it pleases. It is no longer bound by anything.

Dynamoff.description: With so little to eat at high altitudes, it will occasionally even feast on its enemies' brains. It is thought that groups seeking a more dependable food source descended to lower skies, ultimately diverging into .

Tropicaw.description: While some say that it flails around to mimic flowers swaying in the wind and avoid predators, others argue that a crimson flower thrashing violently at high speeds is simply so unsettling that nothing dares approach it.

Flaracle.description: It focuses its mind and reads the future through flames. Should it glimpse an inconvenient destiny, it relentlessly rerolls the prophecy until it gets a favorable result. That is why the future of is always exceptionally bright!

Ophydia.description: Waters where s dwell are often filled with flowers that mimic its form. Many attempts have been made to directly propagate these blooms, but they inevitably wither the moment human hands interfere.

Dupin.description: A copy it created might quietly replace it, leaving even the itself unsure whether it is still the original.

Roujay.description: A thief by nature, it snatches items when no one is looking. No reason to be concerned when caught in the act. Once it swiftly steals your eyes, you won’t be seeing anything anyway.

Venusa.description: Whenever it's confused, it resorts to consulting with the snakes on its head. However, since they're all ultimately the same being, it comes to understand that nothing is understood.

Mycora.description: It views humans as nothing more than convenient parasitic hosts. The reason has taken this form is to deceive humans into lowering their guard, allowing its spores to spread more efficiently.

Loomen.description: Its lantern glows with an eerie light that lays bare the truth of the world. If that light strikes you as blinding, then you most likely have something to hide.

Wistella.description: At the end of its life, a new star appears somewhere in the skies, glowing with the weight of its memories.

Solenne.description: Joy and misery come in equal measure for . Those who choose to take care of one must be prepared for what that entails.

Renjishi.description: At a glance, its movements appear exaggerated and full of openings. Strangely, however, any opponents become overwhelmed, and become unable to look away, as if transfixed.

Aegidron.description: In ancient times, when means of communication were limited, the light within 's wings was used to send simple flash signals.

Grizzbolt.description: With a friendly smile and a hardy physique, it is docile towards one it recognizes as a partner. For reasons unexplained, its personality undergoes a drastic change when wielding a minigun.

Lyleen.description: A docile Pal full of love. It watches over small Pals who have lost their parents. It uses a full-power to discipline naughty Pals.

Orserk.description: It sends electricity into its foes' wounds, roasting them from the inside out. Fights between end in the blink of an eye.

Faleris.description: When it finds its prey it unleashes a whirlwind of flames, burning the entire area to ash. breath is known for its pleasing scent.

Shadowbeak.description: Born from the depths of insanity, its very existence defies reason. Having lost all genetic ties to other Pals, one wonders if it could still even be considered a Pal.

Selyne.description: The object floating behind, resembling the moon, is said to gradually become more circular with each passing generation. No one knows what will happen when the moon is full.

Bastigor.description: The sight of anything round gives it an uncontrollable urge to punch it. For the toughest challenge, after simply living, is making a snowman.

Shaolong.description: Its ring is reserved for a special occasion: upon meeting its destined companion, the rings are traded, a symbol of their eternal partnership.

Silvance.description: Since ancient times, it has remained within the World Tree, continuing its vigil. What it protects, and for what purpose, does not know. After all, the World Tree will tell it everything. The spores it commands surely know who the enemy is.

Dandilord.description: Since ancient times, it has remained within the World Tree, continuing its vigil. What it protects, and for what purpose, does not care. After all, the World Tree knows everything. Only the mist it wields can reveal the truth.

Bellanoir.description: Without existing, she quietly gazed upon the world. One day, she was seized with a longing to be seen. With unbridled lust for vengeance against reality, thus was born the Eclipsed Siren.

Xenolord.description: -Destination reached. Priority: Locate entity |Supersonic Dragon| -Note: Conquest deadline exceeded by years. -Update: No conquest traces found. Naturalization confirmed. Commence elimination protocol. -Staff Memo: Refrain from forgetting or consuming critical data!

Hartalis.description: The silence is unsettling, yet strangely soothing. No one else remains. All have been delivered. O King of Salvation, carry me too into eternal stillness. -The ancient text cuts off here.

Paladius.description: Once one with . Its gleaming form is free of all negative emotions. However, a glimmer of hatred can still be seen deep within its eyes.

Necromus.description: Once one with . Its darkened form is the embodiment of negative emotions. However, a glimmer of compassion can still be seen deep within its eyes.

Frostallion.description: Guardian deity of Palpagos Island, known as the Winter Caller. In the past, when a calamity struck the land, it soared into the sky and sealed away the threat by casting the island into eternal winter.

Neptilius.description: After the sealing of the calamity, a champion from Feybreak emerged. It stares out to sea, awaiting one it deems worthy. For such encounters are no coincidence—they are fate.

Jetragon.description: Watches over Palpagos Island from high above. When calamity returns to the land, the earth will split open, and the skies will burn. It is destined to strike down the calamity in a flash of total destruction.

Panthalus.description: The last of its species, keeping its sacred duty close to heart. Even should its long obligation ever be fulfilled, its cries for its mother would only be answered by a solitary echo.

Astralym.description: A shadow of its former self, this mysterious Pal descended upon the Palpagos long ago. Bringing ruin and calamity, it was sealed away for millennia until it was ultimately defeated by a certain Pal Tamer

Fuack Ignis.description: With a belly built for friction, this Pal sparks flames as it body surfs. But when it gets carried away, it often ends up a rolling fireball.

Celaray Lux.description: Its flashy patterns help it attract a partner. But after a long history of electrocution incidents involving , yellow-and-black stripes are now seen across the island as a sign of danger.

Croajiro Noct.description: The who have forsaken their honor gather and form splinter groups. They will use any underhanded means to take down their chosen prey. If you encounter a pack of in the dead of the night, it's your life that's on the line.

Jolthog Cryst.description: It releases the cold air stored in its body when under attack. The frigid blast it radiates is cold enough to freeze the surrounding air and easily pierces through any attacker's body.

Pengullet Lux.description: The feathers of this Pal have all but disappeared, and it can no longer fly. Yet its longing for the sky has turned to jealousy— so it harnessed electricity to strike down those who still soar.

Penking Lux.description: Its body changed color after gaining electricity to improve underwater hunting. As a result, it lost the affection of — but strangely enough, it now has a loyal follower: .

Kingpaca Cryst.description: With a heart of ice, this Pal is terrible at expressing its emotions. A solitary individual is pitiable, seen as too clumsy in the eyes of a .

Tanzee Ignis.description: Its palms have evolved to handle burning objects without getting scorched. When its gun runs out of ammo, it may grab the heated barrel with its bare hands and use it to club its foes.

Foxparks Cryst.description: It is unskilled at controlling cold air from the moment it is born and tends to choke on the frost it breathes unintentionally. When catches a cold, its nasal discharge freezes, making it difficult to breathe and causing it to panic.

Killamari Primo.description: It sinks its fangs into the enemy’s neck, draining every last drop of blood. When two s latch onto each other, they become locked in a relentless struggle—each trying to suck the other dry until one withers away.

Eikthyrdeer Terra.description: The individual with the hardest horns becomes the leader. Once the horns are lost, so too is its leadership status. It leaves the herd amidst farewell glances and quietly returns to the earth.

Caprity Noct.description: The berries that grow on its back change flavor according to its emotions. The harsher the environment it grows in, the sweeter the berries become. This is why most raised in captivity have never known love.

Turtacle Terra.description: To protect its fragile body, it developed an incredibly tough shell. With most of its senses concentrated in its limbs and shell, it can’t even tell if someone quietly touches its core.

Hangyu Cryst.description: Its gigantic arms can tear through blocks of ice. There were times when great sinners were tied in the town square and had their hair torn out by as a brutal form of public humiliation.

Woolipop Terra.description: Its fur emits a sweet smell, but its meat is disgustingly bitter. Carnivorous Pals lured by its scent into taking a bite will find themselves overwhelmed by the foul taste and pass out.

Azurobe Cryst.description: This Pal’s white ribbon continuously absorbs heat from its surroundings, yet its temperature remains curiously unchanged. This strange ability once made it a target of relentless hunting, leaving it with a deep mistrust of humans to this day.

Kelpsea Ignis.description: Its personality changes depending on the temperature of the water it was born into. born in warm waters generally have a passionate, motivated personality.

Ribbuny Botan.description: Always wears a cheerful smile. Sometimes, thanks to 's pranks, they end up tangled in tentacles and have to fight their way out! Even in tough situations, they keep smiling cheerfully.

Gloopie Primo.description: Be sure to check your plans for the next day before falling asleep with this Pal. Its suction cups leave marks that look suspiciously like bite marks. One sleepy cuddle session could lead to some truly unnecessary concern.

Univolt Cryst.description: It used to be considered an emissary of the Winter God, and thus was not hunted by people. However, after witnesses observed one lose its footing on the ice, its reverence faded, and it quite literally slid into the role of a workhorse.

Gobfin Ignis.description: Long ago, it was a giant and powerful aquatic Pal. However, as food became scarce, it evolved to live on land. Since walking burns many calories, it astonishingly awakened its power to control fire!

Loupmoon Cryst.description: The horns on its head are made of a mysterious ice that never melts. Shaved ice made by breaking off a horn is truly exquisite, but both the one who tries it and end up with a headache.

Elphidran Aqua.description: It possesses a demeanor as pure as its appearance suggests. Perhaps because of this, it harbors no ill will in any of its actions and is indifferent even after killing someone.

Vanwyrm Cryst.description: The melodies of a flute made from the exoskeleton of a are said to cross whole mountain ranges. In ages past, such flutes were used to signal victory in battle.

Polapup Terra.description: Among s, the fastest slider on the snow earns the highest respect. Sometimes, a too-forceful leap leaves one buried in the snow— a common sight during particularly competitive seasons. Traffic forecast: heavy congestion.

Leezpunk Ignis.description: A Pal that has an unusual obsession with their standing posture. Always in search of the hottest pose, this Pal's owner is constantly presented with fervent stances.

Gorirat Terra.description: It beats the ground rhythmically to communicate with its comrades. Stronger than , if every hit the ground at the same time, the island would sink in 1 second.

Surfent Terra.description: Its aerodynamic form is well suited for activities on sand. Poachers often catch them and use them in place of surfboards.

Robinquill Terra.description: A Pal that is very similar to humans who hunt and live in the rocky areas. When bones are found in ruins, there are always human bones found nearby as well.

Katress Ignis.description: Due to its upbringing, it no longer cares about whether its food is raw enough. This Pal stopped worrying about those details, and just prefers its food to be as spicy as possible. Not on good terms with .

Helzephyr Lux.description: It calls forth lightning from the depths of hell. While considered a subspecies of , there's a theory that suggests , struck by lightning from hell, will transform into .

Elgrove Cryst.description: Its frozen horn stays solid through its life. When water begins to drip from this ice, feels the chill of its own life slipping away.

Fenglope Lux.description: In ages past, it was believed that drawing a picture of would result in being struck down by lightning. As time passes, artworks depicting became revered as charms to ward off lightning, making them a common presence.

Dinossom Lux.description: Though struck by lightning, it lives on. The phrase |struck by a 's bolt| has come to mean narrowly escaping death.

Bushi Noct.description: Its body becomes a blade upon death, to be taken up by the next generation. If the who inherits it has a weak heart, the voices of the dead will drive it mad. The madness turns it into that endlessly wanders.

Mammorest Cryst.description: The vegetation on its back varies between individuals. There was a time when seeds of presumed extinct plants were found still frozen on the back of a .

Finsider Ignis.description: Even if you reach into its mouth, there’s nothing inside—it just keeps going. Thinking it might be an interdimensional space, one researcher tried climbing in like a sleeping bag. But when the gag reflex kicked in, they realized... it’s definitely part of the creature’s body.

Petallia Ignis.description: A Pal that burns away into a pile of ash upon the end of its life. The surrounding soil becomes incredibly fertile, allowing an abundance of vegetation to thrive for the following decade.

Incineram Noct.description: It specifically targets baby Pals, taking them back to its domain. One can only imagine the profound despair of a parent whose child has been taken away.

Dazzi Noct.description: Often kind to lonely Pals, it plots to lure them into dropping their guard before striking them down. Yet, unable to find the right moment, it ends up making friends with them instead.

Pyrin Noct.description: It burns mysterious dark matter as energy and expels the remaining particles from its body. If someone rides it, they should take care so as not to gallop down the path of darkness.

Relaxaurus Lux.description: One day, had an idea. Maybe it was about time for a change. Just then, an electric shock raced through its body!

Beakon Cryst.description: Perfectly adapted to colder environments, it hardly feels the cold despite its slender form. Its diving plunge carries tremendous force, as if a giant icicle were dropping straight from the sky.

Ghangler Ignis.description: Its blazing lure doubles as a grill for roasting prey. Drawn in by the delicious aroma, Pals have no hope of escape once sets its sights on them. In the end, they’re treated to a hearty meal—until everyone’s full.

Rayhound Cryst.description: Sprinting across the tundra at full speed kicks up a blinding blizzard, leading to constant collisions due to zero visibility.

Menasting Terra.description: Being made of pure energy, its insides are completely hollow. It stuffs earth and minerals into its outer shell, achieving overwhelming mass. Those who are struck by it can only let out hellish groans.

Needoll Noct.description: Its spikes react to its emotions. A well-loved clings to its owner, but accepting that love means accepting the pain too.

Mossanda Lux.description: A Pal whose power is truly shocking. By altering the electrical currents in its body, this Pal's been able to overload its own strength. When it comes to sheer power, this Pal may be top of the list.

Chillet Ignis.description: It can curl up its body and roll around so fast that sparks fly. Sparks also fly when it feels excited. If you stroke it too much, this pal will get fired up, literally!

Moldron Cryst.description: In a healthy individual, the substance flowing along its back maintains a stable temperature. However, if its heartbeat quickens, the temperature destabilizes, eventually triggering vaporization or crystallization that results in a sudden, abrupt death from shock.

Broncherry Aqua.description: Its scent drastically changes before and after pairing. It exudes a pleasing aroma before finding a partner, which is called the |Perfume of Purity.|

Dumud Gild.description: Its body reflects light with astounding clarity. Even more shocking, its reflexes are somehow even slower than .

Kitsun Noct.description: Despite its appearance, is extremely sensitive and will flee into a cave when spooked. It has become quite gloomy from being a shut-in for too long.

Blazehowl Noct.description: While it prefers raw meat, it always ends up eating tainted meat. This is due to its dark claws, which it uses as its weapon—it simply doesn't realize its prey gets cursed.

Warsect Terra.description: The ultra-hard armor surrounding its body is extremely strong and heat resistant. It doesn't flinch even if a steps on it.

Sibelyx Primo.description: A Pal that loves gazing into the void, and will often remain completely still. It frequently becomes a gathering spot for smaller Pals.

Shroomer Noct.description: The abundance of spores released by this Pal blocks out sunlight. Sometimes, if appears motionless, it's not because it's mimicking a mushroom, but rather because its own spores don't let it see anything.

Jormuntide Ignis.description: Legend says the was once a warrior who, after being wrongly convicted and cast into a volcano, returned as this Pal to annihilate the kingdom.

Suzaku Aqua.description: It was once believed to usher in the rainy season. Whenever there was a flood the previous year, people would relentlessly seek to cull its numbers, hoping to avert disaster in the coming year.

Quivern Botan.description: When you hug , if it smells like sunshine and meadows, tomorrow will be sunny. If it smells damp, tomorrow will be wet and miserable.

Skutlass Ignis.description: A bizarre species which temporarily separates its body and uses its bones to slice through enemies, at the risk of perishing itself upon taking too long. While unconfirmed, older individuals are said to deliberately stay separated in order to trigger their survival instincts and prolong their lifespans.

Reptyro Cryst.description: Ice cold blood runs throughout its body. If heated rapidly, its blood evaporates, causing an immense vapor explosion.

Starryon Primo.description: It takes great pride in its glowing, gorgeous mane. If it gets even slightly dirty, it becomes so mortified it can't focus on running properly. Because skin oils ruin its coat, it wants to avoid being touched too much, even by its favorite Pal Tamer.

Pierdon Cryst.description: It basks in the sun, rotating just enough to melt its ice into its preferred shape. Lopsided ones should be left alone, much like a person who was a little too bold with their sideburns.

Cryolinx Terra.description: It can easily carry many items with its large hands. However, its short legs slow its pace, often leaving it overtaken by nightfall.

Wumpo Botan.description: Researchers once tried to cut the grass off its body to reveal its true form. In the end, only grass was left, as if that was all there was to begin with.

Blazamut Ryu.description: Long ago, it emerged from the depths of the earth. With relentless, raging flames showing no mercy, it burned the entire island until nothing remained. It was known as the Eternal Flame.

Dualith Noct.description: One entity, two minds. They rarely agree and bicker constantly, but once battle begins, mind and body instantly unite as one!

Prixter Lux.description: It pierces foes with its tail to deliver an electric jolt directly into their bodies. However, the current it generates is extremely weak, so a simple punch would do far more damage.

Tetroise Primo.description: Even if the stones on its back were to be removed, will know where they are. No matter how far apart, it will always track them down.

Nitemary Botan.description: What it carries is its own soul. When it falls for someone, it flings its soul at them to merge it with theirs, yearning to become one.

Smokie Cryst.description: The blue flames are merely cool to the touch, but anything left in contact with them slowly freezes. Hold a milk bottle over them, and you get a frozen milkshake!

Whalaska Ignis.description: Determined never to be mistaken for drift ice again, it turned bright red through sheer will. Unfortunately, some Pals can’t see red. So despite its efforts, its personal space continues to be invaded.

Celesdir Noct.description: Where blinding radiance shines, dark shadows are born. It scatters an all-enveloping darkness to reclaim the chaos that ought to be. Balance is what the world requires.

Knocklem Ignis.description: Upon disassembly, it is capable of being reassembled and functioning again. Mixing parts from different individuals will trigger a fatal cognitive error that causes its systems to overheat.

Snock Lux.description: Tossing a heated into the bath for a scorching spa was once a fad of the distant past. Owing to a recent revival, it is now quite literally a hot trend.

Solmora Lux.description: Its corkscrew tail acts as a propeller. Research claims that if its body could withstand the strain, it could generate over 1.21 gigawatts of power.

Eidrolon Ignis.description: A new Pal that was only recently discovered. It trusts nothing and no one, and its tempermental nature leads to it constantly venting its rage at the nearest target.

Lyleen Noct.description: An elegant Pal full of grace. It admonishes any who are disrespectful with a painful slap. Some Pals actively seek out this punishment.

Faleris Aqua.description: When it finds its prey, it unleashes a mighty torrent, sweeping the entire area. More often than not, the prey gets swept away in the chaos, leaving one to wonder whether this truly counts as hunting.

Bellanoir Libero.description: With her desires set loose, she was soon overwhelmed. She longed for a self to gaze upon herself. And the sisters who gazed, resenting reality, were driven into the abyss and, at last, closed their eyes.

Frostallion Noct.description: Guardian deity of Palpagos Island, known as the Night Caller. In the past, when a calamity struck the land, it soared into the sky and sealed away the threat by casting the island into eternal darkness.

Green Slime.description: [Tides of Terraria] A monster from another world, now tameable in this realm.

Blue Slime.description: [Tides of Terraria] A monster from another world, now tameable in this realm.

Red Slime.description: [Tides of Terraria] A monster from another world, now tameable in this realm.

Purple Slime.description: [Tides of Terraria] A monster from another world, now tameable in this realm.

Illuminant Slime.description: [Tides of Terraria] A monster from another world, now tameable in this realm.

Rainbow Slime.description: [Tides of Terraria] A monster from another world, now tameable in this realm.

Enchanted Sword.description: [Tides of Terraria] A monster from another world, now tameable in this realm.

Cave Bat.description: [Tides of Terraria] A monster from another world, now tameable in this realm.

Illuminant Bat.description: [Tides of Terraria] A monster from another world, now tameable in this realm.

Demon Eye.description: [Tides of Terraria] A monster from another world, now tameable in this realm.

Eye of Cthulhu.description: [Tides of Terraria] A monster from another world, now tameable in this realm.

Dragostrophe.description: A silent beast born of the abyss. Thou shall not stand before the beast. Thou shall not heed the beast.

Boltmane.description: The more it moves, the more it heats up and the hair tips increasingly glow brighter. At its peak, it becomes so dazzling that you risk blindness if you stare at it directly.

Ability Glasses.description: Glasses used to discern hidden Pal potentials. Displays Health, Attack and Defense stats on a scale of 0 to 100.

Accumulator.description: A facility for storing excess electricity generated by the Power Generator.

Advanced Chest.description: Stores items. Quite sturdy, with plenty of storage space. It is useful as a large, secure storage box.

Advanced Fishing Rod (Pengullet).description: A fishing rod designed for catching Pals. Use it with bait near water where shadows are visible to start fishing. It boasts improved performance, making catches vastly easier.

Advanced Medicine Workbench.description: A facility for making medicine to cure Pals' illnesses. With advanced technology, it can also create unknown medicines. Assign the task to a Pal capable of pharmacy work.

Advanced Recovery Meds.description: Medicine that heals wounds over time. Extremely high quality, it rapidly restores HP before your eyes.

Alarm Bell.description: Bell that sets the alert status for Pals on base. Change Pal behavior to either attack invading enemies, or focus entirely on work whenever you like.

Alluring Bait.description: Bait that lures in Pals. Improved to enlarge the size of the bar in the fishing minigame and increase the number of items obtained from fishing.

Alpha Wave Generator.description: A device that emits alpha waves to help Pals relax. Slows the rate at which the SAN value of Base Pals decreases. Placing more than one has no additional effect.

Amusement Furniture Set.description: Decorative furniture set. Your Pals will keep on working.

Ancient Civilization Core.description: Core from technology that was lost long ago. Nearly impossible to find on the Palpagos Islands, it contains a wealth of forgotten knowledge.

Ancient Civilization Parts.description: Debris excavated from ancient ruins. Contains abundant information on lost technology. Extremely valuable, people from all walks of life are now seeking such crystals.

Ancient Pal Manuscript.description: An ancient manuscript written on Pal hide. It may be found by Pals during expeditions. Required for advancing research.

Ancient Technical Manual.description: A book that contains long-lost secrets of technology from a forgotten era. Use it to obtain Ancient Technology Points.

Antique Bath Set.description: Decorative furniture set. Can't actually be used to stay clean.

Antique Chair Set.description: Decorative furniture set. Use with an antique desk.

Antique Chest.description: Antique chest for storing items. Adorned with a photo of a Pal.

Antique Couch Set.description: Decorative furniture set. Gives the air of luxury to your base.

Antique Curtain.description: Decorative antique curtain. Can be placed on walls.

Antique Desk Set.description: Decorative furniture set. Use with an antique chair.

Antique Dresser.description: Dresser useful for giving yourself a remake. Allows players to edit their appearance.

Antique High Quality Furniture Set.description: Decorative furniture set. Gives an air of stately elegance to your base.

Antique Lamp Set.description: Antique lamp set to provide illumination. Requires electricity to light.

Antique Mirror Set.description: Decorative furniture set. Essentially for making sure you look good.

Antique Side Chest.description: Antique side chest for storing items. Adorned with a photo of a Pal.

Antique Storage Cabinet Set.description: Antique storage cabinet set. Can store items.

Antique Storage Set.description: Antique storage furniture set. Can store items.

Antique Wall Mirror.description: Decorative antique wall mirror. Can be placed on walls.

Arrow.description: Arrows for use with bows. Can be crafted at Primitive Workbench.

Arrows.description: Arrows for use with bows. Can be crafted at Primitive Workbench.

Assault Rifle.description: An Assault Rifle that mows down enemies with overwhelming force. It excels at rapid-fire and is well suited to taking down powerful foes.

Assault Rifle Ammo.description: Ammo for the Assault Rifle and similar weapons. Can be crafted at Weapon Assembly Line.

Assault Rifle Schematic 1.description: When in inventory, unlocks recipe for Assault Rifle (). Can be crafted at Weapon Assembly Line.

Assault Rifle Schematic 2.description: When in inventory, unlocks recipe for Assault Rifle (). Can be crafted at Weapon Assembly Line.

Average Feed Bag.description: A decently sized pouch for holding food. Unlocks 2 food slots in your inventory. Players and Pals automatically consume food when hungry. Carrying one is enough.

Baked Berries.description: Cooking enhances their nutritional value, and surprisingly, they are quite filling.

Baked Mushroom.description: Cooking this mushroom enhances its nutritional value. Curiously, it's also quite filling.

Barricade Set.description: Decorative furniture set. Place to keep somewhere off limits.

Bat.description: Baseball bat for close-range combat. Might be tough to get a home run with a Pal.

Bathroom Set.description: Decorative furniture set. Can't be used for its original purpose.

Bear Trap (Large).description: A bear trap to immobilize large Pals. The capture rate with the sphere increases when a Pal is trapped. Only effective on large Pals, small Pals will not be caught.

Bear Trap (Small).description: Trap that immobilizes Pals who step on it. The capture rate with the sphere increases when a Pal is trapped. Only effective on small Pals.

Beautiful Flower.description: A plant that blooms colorful flowers. Can be used to make Suspicious Juice and Strange Juice.

Beginner Fishing Set.description: Beginner-friendly fishing rod and bait set. Once you craft both, you'll be able to fish for Pals near bodies of water.

Bellanoir Libero (Ultra) Slab.description: Slab overflowing with 's energy. Offer to a Summoning Altar to summon an inconceivably powerful (Lv ??) .

Bellanoir Libero's Slab.description: Slab depicting . Offer to a Summoning Altar to summon an exceptionally powerful Lv 45 .

Bellanoir Libero's Slab Fragment.description: Fragment depicting . Combine all of them to create a Slab.

Bellanoir's Slab.description: Slab depicting . Offer to a Summoning Altar to summon a powerful Lv 35 .

Bellanoir's Slab Fragment.description: Fragment depicting . Combine all of them to create a Slab.

Berry Plantation.description: A farm that can grow Red Berries. Harvest time is quick, but eating them doesn't provide much sustenance. Requires a few Pals to plant seeds, water, and harvest the crops.

Berry Seeds.description: Seeds that produce Red Berries. Necessary for starting a farm. Can be obtained when picking Red Berries.

Beta Wave Generator.description: A device that emits beta waves to energize Pals. Increases the work speed of Base Pals. Placing more than one has no additional effect.

Big Wall Ivy.description: Decorative large ivy. Can be placed on a wall.

Blazamut Ryu (Ultra) Slab.description: Slab overflowing with energy of . Offer to a Summoning Altar to summon an inconceivably powerful (Lv ??) .

Blazamut Ryu Slab.description: Slab depicting . Offer to a Summoning Altar to summon a very powerful Lv 55 .

Bone.description: Bone collected from Pals. A valuable source of premium calcium.

Boost Gun.description: Firing this at a Pal in your team temporarily boosts that Pal's attack and defense. Firing it at an enemy deals damage instead.

Bow.description: Primitive long range weapon. Hastily put together, it isn't very powerful.

Bowler Hat.description: A tough, round hat. It was sold with the guarantee that it wouldn't break even if a stepped on it.

Bowler Hat Schematic 1.description: When in inventory, unlocks recipe for Bowler Hat (). Can be crafted at Primitive Workbench.

Bread.description: Bread made from baked Flour. A tasty staple food.

Breeding Farm.description: Assign one male and one female Pal to produce eggs. Placing Cake in the facility is required for production.

Brick Fireplace.description: Brick fireplace that provides illumination. Must be lit with fire from a Fire Pal.

Broiled Dumud.description: Grilled fish slathered with a secret sauce. Soothes both mind and body. Perfect for tired Pal Tamers.

Broncherry Meat.description: meat. Giant meat full of massive stores of energy. Stuffed to the brim with nutrients gathered through photosynthesis.

Broncherry Rib Roast.description: Grilled meat brimming with the flavor of the king of the wilds. Juices from the compressed meat flow from the bone, making it surprisingly tender despite the initial toughness.

Burden Elixir.description: Extremely valuable elixir that permanently raises carrying capacity when consumed. Ineffective when used by Pals.

Burden Remedy.description: Extremely valuable potion that permanently raises carrying capacity by a slight amount when consumed. Ineffective when used by Pals.

Cake.description: A dish eaten during celebrations. Put it in the chest at a Breeding Farm to make Pals lay a particularly healthy egg.

Campfire.description: Needed for cooking food. It can only be used to grill simple ingredients. Requires a Fire Pal to light a flame.

Caprity Meat.description: meat. Healthy and full of nutrients. Hints of berries scenting the meat stimulates the appetite.

Carbon Fiber.description: A lightweight yet sturdy material. Used to create armor and other equipment. Can be crafted at Production Assembly Line.

Carbonara.description: A dish mixing pasta with Egg and Milk. Eating it makes it improves by +20% for a period of time.

Carpet Set.description: Decorative furniture set. Place on a foundation in your base for that homey feel.

Carrot.description: A vibrant, nutrient-rich root vegetable. Not suitable for raw consumption.

Carrying Lotus (S).description: Small purple flower that is extremely difficult to obtain. Needed to make Burden Remedy.

Cave Mushroom.description: Mushroom that grows prolifically in caves. Can be made into healing medicine.

Cavern Mushroom.description: Mushroom that grows prolifically in caves. Can be made into healing medicine.

Ceiling Lamp.description: A light source that provides illumination to a base even at night. It can be placed on a ceiling. Requires electricity, but lights up a larger area.

Celaray's Gloves.description: Gloves for modifying the performance of the equipped glider, if is in your team.

Cement.description: Cement used for creating structures and more. Can be produced in a High-Quality Workbench.

Charcoal.description: Charcoal made from burning Wood. It can be combined with Sulfur to create Gunpowder.

Charge Rifle.description: A beam rifle crafted with advanced technology. Charges energy to deliver one powerful shot.

Chikipi Poultry.description: meat. Light and easy to eat, it's a local favorite for residents of Palpagos Island.

Chikipi Sauté.description: Plump poultry sautéed in butter. Accented with fresh lemons. Eating it will slightly increase work speed for a period of time.

Chromite.description: A type of metal buried underground. It can be detected with a Metal Detector on certain islands.

Circuit Board.description: Required to create precision equipment. Can be made with a Production Assembly Line.

Claw Crane.description: Decorative claw crane. Broken, it can't be played.

Clock Set.description: Decorative furniture set. Doesn't actually tell time.

Cloth.description: Cloth woven from Wool. Required to create armor. Can be crafted at Primitive Workbench.

Cloth Covered Container.description: Cloth covered container for storing items.

Cloth Outfit.description: Clothes made from Cloth. Protects against cold nights.

Cloth Outfit Schematic 1.description: When in inventory, unlocks recipe for Cloth Outfit (). Can be crafted at Primitive Workbench.

Cloth Outfit Schematic 3.description: When in inventory, unlocks recipe for Cloth Outfit (). Can be crafted at Primitive Workbench.

Coal.description: Coal often found in caves and other places. Required to create high quality metal.

Coal Mine.description: Facility for producing Coal. Digging Coal is grueling physical work. Leave it to a Pal that is good at mining.

Coarse Ammo.description: Ammo for the Musket, Makeshift Handgun, and similar weapons. Can be crafted at Weapon Workbench.

Cola Vending Machine.description: Decorative cola vending machine. Cannot be used to buy items.

Cold Resistant Metal Armor.description: Improved Metal Armor. Useful for surviving in cold climates.

Cold Resistant Metal Armor Schematic 1.description: When in inventory, unlocks recipe for Cold Resistant Metal Armor (). Can be crafted at High-Quality Workbench.

Cold Resistant Pal Metal Armor.description: Improved Pal Metal Armor. Useful for surviving in cold climates.

Cold Resistant Pelt Armor.description: A Pelt Armor produced in cold regions. Useful for surviving in cold climates.

Cold Resistant Pelt Armor Schematic 1.description: When in inventory, unlocks recipe for Cold Resistant Pelt Armor (). Can be crafted at High-Quality Workbench.

Cold Resistant Refined Metal Armor.description: Improved Refined Metal Armor. Useful for surviving in cold climates.

Cold Resistant Refined Metal Armor Schem.description: When in inventory, unlocks recipe for Cold Resistant Refined Metal Armor (). Can be crafted at Production Assembly Line.

Common Shield.description: Strange shield made with Paldium technology. It automatically repairs itself after not taking damage for a while.

Compound Bow.description: A modernized bow. Capable of firing more powerful arrows.

Cooking Pot.description: Needed for cooking food. Use of a cooking pot allows for a wider variety of meal preparation. Requires a Fire Pal to light a flame.

Cooler.description: A device that blows cool air, lowering the temperature of a base. It doesn't provide much relief from the heat, but helps one to endure somewhat hot environments. Requires an Ice Pal for cooling.

Cooler Box.description: Small food vault. Assign an Ice Pal to help prevent food stored inside from spoiling.

Copper Key.description: A key that can be used to open locked treasure chests.

Cotton Candy.description: Cotton candy collected from . Its sweetness fluffily melts away in your mouth.

Crossbow.description: Fires an Arrow effortlessly. Though it takes time to load, it delivers a powerful shot.

Crossbow Schematic 1.description: When in inventory, unlocks recipe for Crossbow (). Can be crafted at High-Quality Workbench.

Crossbow Schematic 2.description: When in inventory, unlocks recipe for Crossbow (). Can be crafted at High-Quality Workbench.

Crude Oil.description: A material that can be processed into various products. Obtained by installing a Crude Oil Extractor in an oil field.

Crusher.description: Facility for breaking down Stone and Wood to exchange them for other materials. Requires Pal with the Watering trait to spin the water turbine.

Curve Module.description: Equipping it causes the sphere to spin with a curved trajectory. It becomes easier to catch Pals off guard, and increases capture power.

Daedream's Necklace.description: Collar for . If is in your team, it will stay by your side and attack enemies in tandem.

Dark Fragment.description: Dropped by some powerful Pals. Used as a material for crafting powerful accessories and other items.

Dark Grenade.description: A Dark Grenade useful for mid-range combat. It detonates a few seconds after being thrown, dealing damage to enemies.

Dazzi Cloud.description: Cloud dropped from a . Soft and fluffy, it is used to make clothing.

Dazzi's Necklace.description: Collar for . If is in your team, it will stay by your side and attack enemies in tandem.

Decal Ink.description: Ammo for Decal Guns. Uses ink to paint a picture of a Pal. Can be crafted at Weapon Workbench.

Deluxe Bait.description: Bait that lures in Pals. Improved to slightly enlarge the size of the bar in the fishing minigame.

Diamond.description: A rare gemstone that glows brilliantly. Can be sold to a merchant for a high price.

Digtoise's Headband.description: Headband for . Activate its Partner Skill to make spin at high speed, allowing it to more efficiently drill through rock.

Dog Coin.description: A mysterious coin. Apparently there are people who collect these. A will usually have them, but for some reason, they cannot be obtained by butchering it.

Double Street Lamp.description: Double street lamp that provides illumination. Requires electricity to light.

Double-barreled Shotgun.description: Wreaks havoc at close range. Can fire twice before reloading. Despite the limited load capacity, it still packs a punch.

Dragon Grenade.description: A Dragon Grenade useful for mid-range combat. It detonates a few seconds after being thrown, dealing damage to enemies.

Dumud Chowder.description: Dumud lavishly covered in a thick, creamy soup. Eating it will increase work speed and stay full slightly longer for a period of time.

Egg.description: Delicious when fried, but can also be used to cook various meals. produce them.

Egg Incubator.description: Used for incubating a Pal Egg. If a Pal Egg is left in it, it will automatically hatch after some time has passed.

Eikthyrdeer Loco Moco.description: Thick hamburger steak accented with a creamy fried egg. This sublime combination brings pure joy to the mouths of those who eat it. Eating it will increase by +20% for a period of time.

Eikthyrdeer Stew.description: A tender, melt-in-your-mouth venison stew crafted with love. The intertwining flavors of vegetables and meat create a harmonious blend that brings out a comforting richness. Eating it will increase by +20% for a period of time.

Eikthyrdeer Venison.description: meat. Light and lean, but rich in flavor.

Electric Cooler.description: A device that blows cool air, lowering the temperature of a base. Using electricity, it can really lower the temperature. Requires an Ice Pal for cooling.

Electric Egg Incubator.description: Device for incubating Pal eggs. Requires electricity, but automatically keeps the inside of the incubator at appropriate temperatures.

Electric Furnace.description: Allows refinement of Pal Metal Ingot. It requires electricity to use, but is quicker to process. Requires a Fire Pal to light a flame.

Electric Heater.description: A device that blows warm air, raising the temperature of a base. Using electricity, it can really heat things up. Requires a Fire Pal to light a flame.

Electric Kitchen.description: Needed for cooking food. It requires electricity to run, but allows a large amount of meals to be prepared rapidly. Requires a Fire Pal to light a flame.

Electric Medicine Workbench.description: A facility for making Pal medicine. It requires electricity, but can produce high quality medicine. Leave its operation to a Pal that can make medicine.

Electric Mine.description: Trap that electrocutes the Pal that steps on it. The electrocuted Pal has a higher capture rate with the sphere.

Electric Organ.description: Material obtainable from Pals. It stores electricity, but requires caution when used.

Electric Pylon.description: Place in base to improve electricity generating speed. Placing more than one has no additional effect.

Elizabee's Staff.description: A spear made from 's staff, altered for human use. It is imbued with 's indomitable spirit.

Emerald.description: A gemstone that glows green. Can be sold to a merchant for a high price.

Emergency Exit Sign Set.description: Decorative furniture set. Apparently a priceless artifact in more civilized times.

Energy Cartridge.description: Ammo for the Laser Rifle and similar weapons. Can be crafted at Weapon Assembly Line II.

Exotic Sphere.description: An item that captures Pals when thrown. Boasting incredible performance, it can capture Pals that fall outside regular standards.

Exploration Cap Schematic 1.description: When in inventory, unlocks recipe for Explorer Cap (). Can be crafted at Primitive Workbench.

Explorer Cap.description: A hat for explorers. It protects from the bright rays of the sun.

Farming Hat.description: A hat for working in the fields. It protects from the bright rays of the sun.

Farming Hat Schematic 1.description: When in inventory, unlocks recipe for Farming Hat (). Can be crafted at Primitive Workbench.

Feathered Hair Band.description: A beautiful feathered headband. Though improbable, it somehow seems to protect its wearer from lethal head injuries.

Feathered Hair Band Schematic 1.description: When in inventory, unlocks recipe for Feathered Hair Band (). Can be crafted at Primitive Workbench.

Feed Box.description: A box for storing Pal food. Pals can feed from it when hungry. Make sure to keep it well-stocked to keep your Pals happy.

Fiber.description: Fiber collected from trees. A material used when creating bows, etc.

Fine Bed.description: A fluffy bed for humans. Use it when injured or to sleep at night. You won't be able to sleep soundly without a roof over your head.

Fire Arrow.description: Fire Arrows for use with bows. Deals damage on contact. Can be crafted at Primitive Workbench.

Fire Arrow Crossbow.description: A Crossbow that deals damage. Burns Pals with fire arrows, making them easier to capture. Requires Fire Arrow.

Fire Bow.description: A bow that deals damage. Burns Pals with fire arrows, making them easier to capture. Requires Fire Arrow.

Fireplace.description: Fireplace that provides illumination. Must be lit with fire from a Fire Pal.

Fireplace Set.description: Brick fireplace set to provide illumination. Must be lit with fire.

Flame Cauldron.description: Place in base to improve fire-making speed. Placing more than one has no additional effect.

Flame Organ.description: Material obtainable from Pals. Can be used to make Fire Arrow and more.

Flamethrower.description: Flamethrower that fires long-range flame. Can set the enemy on fire.

Flamethrower Fuel.description: Ammo for the Flamethrower and similar weapons. Can be crafted at Weapon Assembly Line II.
